Video.summary: In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. Whilst traversing steep mountains in the company of Kodiak brown bears and taking on cold Alaskan waters, Andy presents useful information for fellow outdoor enthusiasts all whilst building friendships with the great people of Kodiak Big Timber Lodge.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alaska"

Video.summary: Motorcycle junkie and Army veteran Dave Allen recounts the terrifying prospect of facing chemical warfare during Operation Desert Storm with host Jay Garstecki and special guest Paul Sr. from American Choppers.
Video.title: "Dave Allen"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike and Capt. Vincent Sabatella catch some big Grouper in the shallow waters of Citrus County.
Video.title: "Citrus County Grouper"

Video.summary: Mike visits Crystal River and fishes with one of his Citrus County friends, Capt JC Livingston, where they battle the good, the bad and the ugly that comes with Tarpon fishing!
Video.title: "100 Ways To Lose A Tarpon"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela is on a mission to better understand how wild-harvested protein contributes economically and culturally to society in North America. Join him as he learns more about the Wild Harvest Initiative and sets out to harvest his own food for the freezer.
Video.title: "Wild Harvest"

Video.summary: Rush teams up with longtime friend and fellow angler Jesus Diaz for a well-earned "busman's holiday" in the beautiful waters of Key West. Even on a day off, these two can't resist seeing what's going to bite. Taking the wind and the tides into account, the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day "off" work.
Video.title: "Fishing With Zeus in Key West"

Video.summary: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture. Show host, Dan Cabela, participates in a scientific sampling project to monitor the - population's health, and get first-hand experience of the relationship between local people and this arctic icon.
Video.title: "Barren Ground Musk Ox"

Video.summary: Huckberry's flagship adventure series is back in Japan and the DIRT crew is hungry as ever for adventure. This time, they’re diving into the Kansai region—where millennia-old traditions flow from the shore of Lake Biwa to surf breaks on the coast. Chef Coleman Griffin gives them a taste of the deep cultural immersion that inspires his culinary practice, from chef knives descended from samurai swords to fermented carp that went on to spawn sushi. And turns out, the perfect meal does exist, and the crew knew it as soon as they had a bite of their final omakase.
Video.title: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Video.summary: Shot through the neck on Fort Hood in Texas, Green Beret John Arroyo recounts coming face to face with his attacker and Jesus blinding his would be killer.
Video.title: "John Arroyo"

Video.summary: Capt. Jonathan Moss ventures to South Florida with the hope of having a few Exotic Encounters.
Video.title: "Exotic Encounters"

Video.summary: Captain Mike Anderson and Captain Sebastian Velez get on a great Trout bite in their home waters.
Video.title: "Backyard Trout"

Video.summary: Capt. Jonathan Moss is heading offshore into the Gulf of America, eager to feed his offshore fishing obsession.
Video.title: "Offshore Obsession"

Video.summary: This episode of Huckberry Homes brings us out to the Western Slope, just a few miles outside of Vail, Colorado to visit our longtime friend and collaborator Sinuhe Xavier. When he's not traveling the world shooting commercials and making short films, Xavier's at home in his quiet, perfectly appointed retreat deep in the Rockies here he's a three hour drive from Red Rock desert and a ten minute drive from some of the best snowboarding in the world. There was plenty of magic in Sinuhe's spot, from his BMW GS to his exploration of how his love for his son shaped the design and decoration of his pad.
Video.title: "Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"

Image.alt: "Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"
"Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"
"Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"

Video.summary: U.S. Marines combat correspondent Ken Plant tells of being captured and tortured as a POW.
Video.title: "Ken Plant"

Video.summary: An Honor No One Wants: A Gold Star Family is the immediate family member(s) of a fallen soldier who died while serving in a time of conflict. Ginger Gilbert-Ravella honors the memory her husband…American hero Troy Gilbert.
Video.title: "Ginger Gilbert-Ravella"

Video.summary: Join Mike on this special adventure into some of the best locations Citrus County has to offer.
Video.title: "Citrus County Collection"

Video.summary: Captain Mike Anderson and Captain Ozzie Lessinger take advantage of the springtime bite at South Seas Island resort.
Video.title: "South Seas Springtime"

Video.summary: Despite adverse conditions, Capt. Jonathan Moss and long time friend, Capt. Adam Whitson, are heading out on the water in search of the Crystal River Inshore Slam.
Video.title: "Weather or Not"

Video.summary: Central Florida’s St. John’s River is home to countless species of wildlife, making this sweet water estuary the perfect playground for a pair of river rats, Captains Jonathan Moss and Nick Clark.
Video.title: "River Rats"

Video.summary: With Montana in the rearview mirror the Flylords team crosses over into Idaho. In this leg of the trip they hit Blue-Ribbon trout waters with the folks from Henrys Fork Anglers and Silver Creek Outfitters. They also have a chance to sit down with the owners of these operations and hear what these shops and fly fishing mean to them. Before they hit the road to Utah the crew joins in on another conservation event at Payette Brewing Company.
Video.title: "SIGHT Casting RISING Trout in IDAHO"

Video.summary: Establishing themselves just after the last Ice Age, the blacktail of Vancouver Island offers an important genetic population isolated from the mainland. To explore the local relationship with the species, its harvesting, conservation and history, Dan Cabela travels to the island.
Video.title: "The Blacktail of Vancouver Island"

Video.summary: Famous for its Mayan ruins, the Yucatan in Mexico is home to a diverse array of wildlife. After years of over-harvesting and exploitation, regulated hunting is helping to conserve large areas of this fragile ecosystem. Dan Cabela travels to the region to find out more.
Video.title: "Into the Yucatán"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair has some down time in Alaska, so what does he do? Heads to the beach to check out the salmon hatchery in Ketchican. They've met their quota for the year so the season is open, but the trick is how to catch the salmon. There hasn't been an episode like this yet.
Video.title: "Salmon Snagging"

Video.summary: When the captain calls and says the weather in Alaska is great, you don't waste the opportunity. So Capt. Blair and the whole crew head out for a day of fishing. With one camera on the boat, things got a little chaotic with all the catching going on.
Video.title: "Weather Window Day"

Video.summary: After multiple days in Alaska, it was time for the last trip out to sea. The crew got to experience the sights on this trip, like God Valley and others. And of course catch more Alaskan fish.
Video.title: "The Final Voyage"

Video.summary: Down in Sebastion, the monster snook wait. These fish are so elusive, men have gotten divorced chasing these things. These can be once in a lifetime fish, and even then it's not a guarantee.
Video.title: "Hunting a Mogan"

Video.summary: With Meredith McCord halfway around the world, Capt. Justin Leake gives show producer Chris Hermans his first shot at landing a tarpon after filming them for 9 seasons. Does art imitate life?
Video.title: "Tarpon Producer"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin Leake joins some old friends on board the Fall Line in hopes of scoring big in the Bay Point Billfish Open. This trip will test the limits of all the anglers as the skilled captain and crew do the impossible in hopes of bringing home the prize.
Video.title: "Billfish Tourney"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin and Florida WaterSports pro Jeff Lange go deep off Panama City Beach to test the limits of a new battery powered reel. The results are surprising.
Video.title: "Electric Deep Drop"

Video.summary: When tarpon don’t cooperate, it’s time for Capt. Justin and friend Ethan Register to have a competition for dinner options in the gulf’s grocery store.
Video.title: "Once Upon a Wahoo"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Saltwater Experience Charters’ Captain Garrett Stephens for a trip into the Gulf. When their catches get repeatedly cut off, they decide to bring the massive bull sharks up to the surface for a closer look.
Video.title: "Gulf Monsters With Captain Garrett Stephens"

Video.summary: After wrestling with some massive bull sharks, Captains Tom Rowland, Rich Tudor and guest Captain Garrett Stephens encounter some crazy cobia action and it takes all their strength and skill to keep them away from the sharks.
Video.title: "Cobia Fishing With Garrett Stephens"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor utilize the Yellowfin Hybrid 26’ to bring the Live Paddle Boards out to an Islamorada flat for a fun day of fishing for bonefish and some toothy critters.
Video.title: "Islamorada Paddleboard Fishing"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor run west from Hawks Cay to fish the spring tarpon migration at the legendary Bahia Honda bridge.
Video.title: "Bahia Honda Tarpon"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head down to Key West to join Captain Brandon Cyr for some skinny water bonefishing.
Video.title: "Bonefishing Key West with Captain Brandon Cyr"

Image.alt: "Bonefishing Key West with Captain Brandon Cyr"
"Bonefishing Key West with Captain Brandon Cyr"
"Bonefishing Key West with Captain Brandon Cyr"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor fish the Lower Keys with Captain Brandon Cyr for baby tarpon in the mangroves.
Video.title: "Baby Tarpon with Captain Brandon Cyr"

Video.summary: The Southards continue their family tradition on Table Rock Lake. While they’re there, Trenton spends a day with “Mr. Worldwide” James Watson and learns some things from one of the best anglers on the lake.
Video.title: "Table Rock Worldwide"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve catch a mixed bag bottom fishing off of Islamorada with Captain Scott Stoky.
Video.title: "Islamorada Mixed Bag"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve go yellow tailing out of Hawks Cay for a limit of tasty yellowtail snapper and Steve picks a battle with a big lemon shark.
Video.title: "Yellowtails And Lemons At Hawks Cay"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve bounce around the reefs out of Hawks Cay catching a wide variety of reef dwellers.
Video.title: "Reef Fishing Out Of Hawks Cay"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve take their brand new Invincible 36’ out of Plantation Boat Mart to fly the kites for some early season sailfishing.
Video.title: "Sailfish Season"

Video.summary: Join Rush and his good friend Anthony Solmo for an epic day of fishing in the stunning waters of Key West, Florida! Leaving the Odyssea at the dock, they hop on a bay boat outfitted with a trolling motor for the ultimate inshore adventure. Gliding quietly through the shallows, they target a variety of species—including hard-pulling Tarpon and explosive topwater Jacks—leaving Rush thinking, “A guy could get used to this kind of fishing!”
Video.title: "Bay Day"

Video.summary: SoCal summer is calling, and the Local Knowledge crew is back on Ali’s home turf in San Diego, California. With reports of hot bites and fish moving in, the crew faces a classic Southern California dilemma — chase big yellowtail around the Coronado Islands or head north in search of bluefin tuna. The options are plenty, the challenges are real, and that’s exactly what keeps fishing in SoCal so exciting.
Video.title: "Dog Gone Yellow"

Video.summary: When the yellowtail at the islands refuse to play, it’s time to switch up the game plan. Ali and Rush pivot north of San Diego in search of hard-charging bluefin tuna during a multi-day mission aboard the 43’ custom ACI Catamaran "Killer Bee". Armed with the right crew, the right gear, and a never-quit mindset, the guys roll the dice on an overnight move—hoping to wake up to redemption and put a few quality bluefin on deck.
Video.title: "Killer Bee"

Video.summary: Returning to Venice, Louisiana for the first time in nearly a decade, Rush reconnects with longtime friends Eric and Monique Newman of Journey South Outfitters for an unforgettable inshore fishing adventure in the heart of the Mississippi River Delta. With reports of massive schools of redfish cruising the beaches, Monique (“Moe”) must decide whether to trust the intel and make a bold run down the coast—or rely on her own instincts built from years on the water. The mission is simple: put Rush on a true bull redfish and see if he has what it takes to earn a spot in her coveted “Over 40-Inch Club.” This trip highlights why Venice, Louisiana is considered one of the premier inshore fisheries in the country—and why having the right guide makes all the difference. Target Species: Bull Redfish. Location: Venice, Louisiana – Mississippi Delta. Outfitter: Journey South Outfitters.
Video.title: "Moe On Mission"

Video.summary: In this episode we will dive the beautiful reefs and wrecks of Key Largo. We will showcase research that is being done in Key Largo to protect reefs worldwide.
Video.title: "Key Largo"

Video.summary: As a child, Piotr Chara spent countless hours on the Oder River in Poland, fishing with his father and wondering at the wildlife; as he grew, Piotr noticed a decline in the river’s bird species and decided to take action. Now an award-winning photographer - and a father himself - Piotr runs an organization that builds floating nests for the birds and restores the rapidly receding wetlands.
Video.title: "Wings Of The Oder"

Video.summary: Nathalie Lasselin is a diver, filmmaker and activist who has explored everything from the Arctic to underwater caves in China; yet this River Hero finds herself pulled back to the waters of her home, Montreal, to help clean up the St. Lawrence River. Underwater, Lasselin and her team scuba dive to collect water samples for testing and scavenge for manmade debris, removing up to six tons of trash each year from a river that is the source of drinking water for so many Canadians.
Video.title: "Natalie Lasselin's Deep Dive"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and his former mate Captain Chris Walter fish the shallow patch reefs of Hawk Channel on a windy day.
Video.title: "Fishing Shallow on a Windy Day in Hawk Channel with Captain Chris Walter"

Image.alt: "Fishing Shallow on a Windy Day in Hawk Channel with Captain Chris Walter"
"Fishing Shallow on a Windy Day in Hawk Channel with Captain Chris Walter"
"Fishing Shallow on a Windy Day in Hawk Channel with Captain Chris Walter"
